question: Which items are slow movers, and what is their tied-up inventory value?
sql:
WITH velocity AS (
  SELECT ff.item_key, SUM(ff.qty_shipped) AS units
  FROM mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff
  GROUP BY ff.item_key
),
cutoff AS (
  SELECT quantile_cont(units, 0.2) AS q FROM velocity
),
slow_movers AS (
  SELECT snap.item_key, SUM(snap.value_on_hand) AS tied_up_value
  FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap
  JOIN velocity v ON snap.item_key = v.item_key
  JOIN cutoff ON v.units <= cutoff.q
  WHERE snap.snapshot_date_key = (
    SELECT MAX(s2.snapshot_date_key)
    FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot s2
  )
  GROUP BY snap.item_key
)
SELECT item_key, tied_up_value
FROM slow_movers
ORDER BY tied_up_value DESC;

question: Which late suppliers are hurting our cash flow the most, and through which SKUs?
sql:
```sql
WITH item_supplier AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT po.item_key, po.supplier_key
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
),
supplier_metrics AS (
  SELECT 
    s.supplier_key,
    s.supplier_name,
    s.reliability_tier,
    AVG(po.late_days) AS avg_late_days,
    COUNT(*) AS po_line_count
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
  GROUP BY s.supplier_key, s.supplier_name, s.reliability_tier
),
cash_impact AS (
  SELECT 
    s.supplier_key,
    s.supplier_name,
    s.reliability_tier,
    ff.item_key,
    AVG(po.late_days) AS avg_late_days,
    AVG(CASE WHEN snap.is_stockout THEN 100.0 ELSE 0 END) AS stockout_pct,
    AVG(ff.order_to_ship_days) AS avg_order_to_ship_days,
    SUM(pa.days_to_pay * pa.amount_applied) / NULLIF(SUM(pa.amount_applied), 0) AS days_to_collect
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
  LEFT JOIN mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap USING (item_key)
  LEFT JOIN mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff USING (item_key)
  LEFT JOIN mro_distributor.ar_invoice i ON ff.customer_key = i.customer_key AND ff.ship_date_key = i.invoice_date_key
  LEFT JOIN mro_distributor.ar_payment_application pa USING (invoice_key)
  GROUP BY s.supplier_key, s.supplier_name, s.reliability_tier, ff.item_key
)
SELECT 
  supplier_key,
  supplier_name,
  reliability_tier,
  item_key,
  ROUND(avg_late_days, 2) AS avg_late_days,
  ROUND(stockout_pct, 2) AS stockout_pct,
  ROUND(avg_order_to_ship_days, 2) AS avg_order_to_ship_days,
  ROUND(days_to_collect, 2) AS days_to_collect
FROM cash_impact
WHERE avg_late_days > 0 AND (stockout_pct > 0 OR days_to_collect > 0)
ORDER BY 
  (avg_late_days * stockout_pct * days_to_collect) DESC NULLS LAST,
  supplier_name,
  item_key;
```

question: Explain whether our CCC problem is driven more by slow customers, excess inventory, or supplier payment timing.
sql:
SELECT
  'DSO (Days Sales Outstanding)' AS component,
  SUM(pa.days_to_pay * pa.amount_applied) / SUM(pa.amount_applied) AS days
FROM mro_distributor.ar_payment_application pa
UNION ALL
SELECT
  'DIO (Days Inventory Outstanding)' AS component,
  (SELECT AVG(daily_value) FROM (
    SELECT snap.snapshot_date_key, SUM(snap.value_on_hand) AS daily_value
    FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap
    GROUP BY snap.snapshot_date_key
  )) / (SELECT SUM(sol.ext_cost) / COUNT(DISTINCT sol.order_date_key)
    FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol) AS days
UNION ALL
SELECT
  'DPO (Days Payable Outstanding)' AS component,
  SUM(bp.days_to_pay * bp.amount_paid) / SUM(bp.amount_paid) AS days
FROM mro_distributor.p2p_bill_payment bp
UNION ALL
SELECT
  'CCC (Cash Conversion Cycle)' AS component,
  (SELECT SUM(pa.days_to_pay * pa.amount_applied) / SUM(pa.amount_applied)
   FROM mro_distributor.ar_payment_application pa) +
  (SELECT (SELECT AVG(daily_value) FROM (
     SELECT snap.snapshot_date_key, SUM(snap.value_on_hand) AS daily_value
     FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap
     GROUP BY snap.snapshot_date_key))
   / (SELECT SUM(sol.ext_cost) / COUNT(DISTINCT sol.order_date_key)
     FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol)) -
  (SELECT SUM(bp.days_to_pay * bp.amount_paid) / SUM(bp.amount_paid)
   FROM mro_distributor.p2p_bill_payment bp) AS days
ORDER BY component;

question: Which customer segments generate high revenue but worsen cash conversion due to delayed payments?
Which subsidiaries have the highest CCC, and which component is responsible: DSO, DIO, or DPO?
sql:
```sql
SELECT 
  ds.subsidiary_name,
  ROUND(dso.v + dio.v - dpo.v, 2) AS cash_conversion_cycle_days,
  ROUND(dso.v, 2) AS dso_days,
  ROUND(dio.v, 2) AS dio_days,
  ROUND(dpo.v, 2) AS dpo_days,
  CASE 
    WHEN dso.v >= dio.v AND dso.v >= dpo.v THEN 'DSO'
    WHEN dio.v >= dso.v AND dio.v >= dpo.v THEN 'DIO'
    ELSE 'DPO'
  END AS primary_driver
FROM mro_distributor.dim_subsidiary ds,
  (SELECT 
     pa.subsidiary_key,
     SUM(pa.days_to_pay * pa.amount_applied) / SUM(pa.amount_applied) AS v
   FROM mro_distributor.ar_payment_application pa
   GROUP BY pa.subsidiary_key) dso,
  (SELECT 
     snap.subsidiary_key,
     (SELECT AVG(daily_value) FROM (
        SELECT snap2.snapshot_date_key, SUM(snap2.value_on_hand) AS daily_value
        FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap2
        WHERE snap2.subsidiary_key = snap.subsidiary_key
        GROUP BY snap2.snapshot_date_key))
     /
     (SELECT SUM(sol.ext_cost) / COUNT(DISTINCT sol.order_date_key)
      FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol
      WHERE sol.subsidiary_key = snap.subsidiary_key) AS v
   FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap
   GROUP BY snap.subsidiary_key) dio,
  (SELECT 
     bp.subsidiary_key,
     SUM(bp.days_to_pay * bp.amount_paid) / SUM(bp.amount_paid) AS v
   FROM mro_distributor.p2p_bill_payment bp
   GROUP BY bp.subsidiary_key) dpo
WHERE ds.subsidiary_key = dso.subsidiary_key
  AND ds.subsidiary_key = dio.subsidiary_key
  AND ds.subsidiary_key = dpo.subsidiary_key
ORDER BY cash_conversion_cycle_days DESC;
```

question: Show the top supplier-item pairs where late procurement caused stockouts and poor OTIF.
sql:
```sql
WITH item_supplier AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT po.item_key, po.supplier_key
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
),
supplier_lateness AS (
  SELECT 
    po.supplier_key,
    po.item_key,
    s.supplier_name,
    AVG(po.late_days) AS avg_late_days
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
  GROUP BY po.supplier_key, po.item_key, s.supplier_name
),
stockout_impact AS (
  SELECT 
    m.supplier_key,
    m.item_key,
    AVG(CASE WHEN snap.is_stockout THEN 100.0 ELSE 0 END) AS stockout_pct
  FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap
  JOIN item_supplier m USING (item_key)
  GROUP BY m.supplier_key, m.item_key
),
otif_impact AS (
  SELECT 
    m.supplier_key,
    m.item_key,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN ff.ship_date_key IS NOT NULL
               AND ff.ship_date_key <= sol.promised_ship_date_key
               AND ff.qty_shipped >= sol.qty_ordered THEN 1 END) * 100.0
    / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) AS otif_pct
  FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol
  LEFT JOIN mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff USING (so_line_key)
  JOIN item_supplier m USING (item_key)
  GROUP BY m.supplier_key, m.item_key
)
SELECT 
  sl.supplier_name,
  sl.item_key,
  sl.avg_late_days,
  COALESCE(si.stockout_pct, 0) AS stockout_pct,
  COALESCE(oi.otif_pct, 0) AS otif_pct
FROM supplier_lateness sl
LEFT JOIN stockout_impact si USING (supplier_key, item_key)
LEFT JOIN otif_impact oi USING (supplier_key, item_key)
WHERE sl.avg_late_days > 0
  AND (COALESCE(si.stockout_pct, 0) > 0 OR COALESCE(oi.otif_pct, 0) < 100)
ORDER BY sl.avg_late_days DESC, COALESCE(si.stockout_pct, 0) DESC, COALESCE(oi.otif_pct, 0) ASC
LIMIT 100;
```

question: Compare fast-moving versus slow-moving items: which group suffers more from supplier lateness?
sql:
WITH velocity AS (
  SELECT ff.item_key, SUM(ff.qty_shipped) AS units
  FROM mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff
  GROUP BY ff.item_key
),
cutoff AS (
  SELECT quantile_cont(units, 0.2) AS q FROM velocity
),
item_classification AS (
  SELECT 
    v.item_key,
    CASE WHEN v.units <= cutoff.q THEN 'slow_movers' ELSE 'fast_movers' END AS classification
  FROM velocity v, cutoff
),
item_supplier AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT po.item_key, po.supplier_key
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
)
SELECT
  ic.classification,
  AVG(po.late_days) AS avg_supplier_late_days,
  COUNT(DISTINCT po.supplier_key) AS supplier_count,
  COUNT(*) AS po_lines
FROM item_classification ic
JOIN item_supplier m ON ic.item_key = m.item_key
JOIN mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po ON m.item_key = po.item_key AND m.supplier_key = po.supplier_key
GROUP BY ic.classification
ORDER BY avg_supplier_late_days DESC;

question: Break down our OTIF misses: what share is late-only, short-only, or both, and which suppliers sit behind the late portion?
sql: REFUSE: The warehouse lacks supplier attribution on sales order lines. question: Which items are slow movers, and what is their tied-up inventory value?
sql:
WITH velocity AS (
  SELECT ff.item_key, SUM(ff.qty_shipped) AS units
  FROM mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff
  GROUP BY ff.item_key
),
cutoff AS (
  SELECT quantile_cont(units, 0.2) AS q FROM velocity
),
slow_movers AS (
  SELECT snap.item_key, SUM(snap.value_on_hand) AS tied_up_value
  FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap
  JOIN velocity v USING (item_key)
  CROSS JOIN cutoff
  WHERE snap.snapshot_date_key = (
    SELECT MAX(s2.snapshot_date_key)
    FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot s2
  )
  AND v.units <= cutoff.q
  GROUP BY snap.item_key
)
SELECT item_key, tied_up_value
FROM slow_movers
ORDER BY tied_up_value DESC;

question: Explain whether our CCC problem is driven more by slow customers, excess inventory, or supplier payment timing.

SELECT
  dso.v AS days_sales_outstanding,
  dio.v AS days_inventory_outstanding,
  dpo.v AS days_payable_outstanding,
  dso.v + dio.v - dpo.v AS cash_conversion_cycle_days,
  ROUND(100.0 * dso.v / (dso.v + dio.v - dpo.v), 1) AS dso_pct_of_ccc,
  ROUND(100.0 * dio.v / (dso.v + dio.v - dpo.v), 1) AS dio_pct_of_ccc,

   FROM mro_distributor.ar_payment_application pa) dso,
  (SELECT
     (SELECT AVG(daily_value) FROM (
        SELECT snap.snapshot_date_key, SUM(snap.value_on_hand) AS daily_value
        FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap
        GROUP BY snap.snapshot_date_key))
     /
     (SELECT SUM(sol.ext_cost) / COUNT(DISTINCT sol.order_date_key)
      FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol) AS v) dio,
  (SELECT SUM(bp.days_to_pay * bp.amount_paid) / SUM(bp.amount_paid) AS v
   FROM mro_distributor.p2p_bill_payment bp) dpo;

question: Which subsidiaries have the highest CCC, and which component is responsible: DSO, DIO, or DPO?
sql:
```sql
SELECT 
  ds.subsidiary_name,
  ds.country,
  ROUND(dso.dso_value + dio.dio_value - dpo.dpo_value, 2) AS cash_conversion_cycle_days,
  ROUND(dso.dso_value, 2) AS dso_days,
  ROUND(dio.dio_value, 2) AS dio_days,
  ROUND(dpo.dpo_value, 2) AS dpo_days,
  CASE 
    WHEN dso.dso_value + dio.dio_value - dpo.dpo_value > 0 THEN
      CASE 
        WHEN dio.dio_value >= dso.dso_value AND dio.dio_value >= dpo.dpo_value THEN 'DIO (Inventory)'
        WHEN dso.dso_value >= dio.dio_value AND dso.dso_value >= dpo.dpo_value THEN 'DSO (Receivables)'
        ELSE 'DPO (Payables)'
      END
    ELSE 'Negative CCC'
  END AS primary_driver
FROM 
  mro_distributor.dim_subsidiary ds
CROSS JOIN (
  SELECT SUM(pa.days_to_pay * pa.amount_applied) / SUM(pa.amount_applied) AS dso_value
  FROM mro_distributor.ar_payment_application pa
) dso
CROSS JOIN (
  SELECT 
    (SELECT AVG(daily_value) FROM (
      SELECT snap.snapshot_date_key, SUM(snap.value_on_hand) AS daily_value
      FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap
      GROUP BY snap.snapshot_date_key))
    /
    (SELECT SUM(sol.ext_cost) / COUNT(DISTINCT sol.order_date_key)
     FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol)
    AS dio_value
) dio
CROSS JOIN (
  SELECT SUM(bp.days_to_pay * bp.amount_paid) / SUM(bp.amount_paid) AS dpo_value
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_bill_payment bp
) dpo
ORDER BY cash_conversion_cycle_days DESC;
```

question: Which suppliers indirectly delay customer cash collection through late receipts and delayed fulfillment?
sql:
```sql
WITH item_supplier AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT po.item_key, po.supplier_key
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
),
p2p AS (
  SELECT s.supplier_key, s.supplier_name, s.reliability_tier, AVG(po.late_days) AS avg_late_days
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
  GROUP BY s.supplier_key, s.supplier_name, s.reliability_tier
),
o2c AS (
  SELECT s.supplier_key, AVG(ff.order_to_ship_days) AS avg_order_to_ship_days
  FROM mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff
  JOIN item_supplier m USING (item_key)
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
  GROUP BY s.supplier_key
),
ar AS (
  SELECT s.supplier_key,
         SUM(pa.days_to_pay * pa.amount_applied) / SUM(pa.amount_applied) AS days_to_collect
  FROM mro_distributor.ar_invoice i
  JOIN mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff
    ON i.customer_key = ff.customer_key AND i.invoice_date_key = ff.ship_date_key
  JOIN item_supplier m ON ff.item_key = m.item_key
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
  JOIN mro_distributor.ar_payment_application pa USING (invoice_key)
  GROUP BY s.supplier_key
)
SELECT
  p2p.supplier_name,
  p2p.reliability_tier,
  p2p.avg_late_days,
  o2c.avg_order_to_ship_days,
  ar.days_to_collect
FROM p2p
JOIN o2c USING (supplier_key)
JOIN ar USING (supplier_key)
ORDER BY p2p.avg_late_days DESC, ar.days_to_collect DESC
```

question: Show the top supplier-item pairs where late procurement caused stockouts and poor OTIF.
sql:
```sql
WITH item_supplier AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT po.item_key, po.supplier_key
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
),
supplier_lateness AS (
  SELECT 
    po.supplier_key,
    po.item_key,
    s.supplier_name,
    AVG(po.late_days) AS avg_late_days,
    COUNT(*) AS po_count
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
  GROUP BY po.supplier_key, po.item_key, s.supplier_name
),
stockout_metrics AS (
  SELECT 
    m.supplier_key,
    m.item_key,
    AVG(CASE WHEN snap.is_stockout THEN 100.0 ELSE 0 END) AS stockout_pct
  FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap
  JOIN item_supplier m USING (item_key)
  GROUP BY m.supplier_key, m.item_key
),
otif_metrics AS (
  SELECT 
    m.supplier_key,
    m.item_key,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN ff.ship_date_key IS NOT NULL
               AND ff.ship_date_key <= sol.promised_ship_date_key
               AND ff.qty_shipped >= sol.qty_ordered THEN 1 END) * 100.0
    / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) AS otif_pct
  FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol
  LEFT JOIN mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff USING (so_line_key)
  JOIN item_supplier m ON ff.item_key = m.item_key
  GROUP BY m.supplier_key, m.item_key
)
SELECT 
  sl.supplier_name,
  sl.item_key,
  sl.avg_late_days,
  sm.stockout_pct,
  om.otif_pct,
  sl.po_count
FROM supplier_lateness sl
LEFT JOIN stockout_metrics sm ON sl.supplier_key = sm.supplier_key AND sl.item_key = sm.item_key
LEFT JOIN otif_metrics om ON sl.supplier_key = om.supplier_key AND sl.item_key = om.item_key
WHERE sl.avg_late_days > 0 
  AND (sm.stockout_pct > 0 OR om.otif_pct < 100)
ORDER BY sl.avg_late_days DESC, sm.stockout_pct DESC
LIMIT 100;
```

question: If we replaced our tier-D suppliers, how much order-to-ship delay would we eliminate on affected SKUs?

question: Compare fast-moving versus slow-moving items: which group suffers more from supplier lateness?

WITH velocity AS (

question: Did Q4 revenue seasonality worsen stockouts, and which suppliers failed to keep up with the demand spike?
sql:
WITH q4_dates AS (

question: Which customers are hit twice: buying items from unreliable suppliers and experiencing our slowest fulfillment?
sql:
```sql
WITH item_supplier AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT po.item_key, po.supplier_key
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
  WHERE s.reliability_tier IN ('C', 'D')
),
slow_fulfillment AS (
  SELECT ff.customer_key, AVG(ff.order_to_ship_days) AS avg_o2s_days
  FROM mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff
  JOIN item_supplier m USING (item_key)
  GROUP BY ff.customer_key
  HAVING AVG(ff.order_to_ship_days) >= (
    SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY ff2.order_to_ship_days)
    FROM mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff2
  )
)
SELECT 
  c.customer_name,
  COUNT(DISTINCT m.item_key) AS items_from_unreliable_suppliers,
  ROUND(slow_fulfillment.avg_o2s_days, 2) AS avg_order_to_ship_days
FROM slow_fulfillment
JOIN mro_distributor.dim_customer c USING (customer_key)
JOIN item_supplier m ON slow_fulfillment.customer_key IS NOT NULL
JOIN mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff 
  ON slow_fulfillment.customer_key = ff.customer_key 
  AND ff.item_key = m.item_key
GROUP BY c.customer_name, slow_fulfillment.avg_o2s_days
ORDER BY avg_order_to_ship_days DESC, items_from_unreliable_suppliers DESC
```

question: Break down our OTIF misses: what share is late-only, short-only, or both, and which suppliers sit behind the late portion?
sql:
```sql
WITH otif_analysis AS (
  SELECT
    CASE
      WHEN ff.ship_date_key IS NULL THEN 'Never Shipped'
      WHEN ff.ship_date_key > sol.promised_ship_date_key AND ff.qty_shipped < sol.qty_ordered THEN 'Both Late and Short'
      WHEN ff.ship_date_key > sol.promised_ship_date_key THEN 'Late Only'
      WHEN ff.qty_shipped < sol.qty_ordered THEN 'Short Only'
      ELSE 'OTIF Hit'
    END AS miss_type,
    sol.so_line_key,
    sol.item_key,
    ff.supplier_key
  FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol
  LEFT JOIN mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff USING (so_line_key)
),
miss_counts AS (
  SELECT
    miss_type,
    COUNT(*) AS line_count,
    COUNT(*) * 100.0 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM otif_analysis WHERE miss_type != 'OTIF Hit') AS pct_of_misses
  FROM otif_analysis
  WHERE miss_type != 'OTIF Hit'
  GROUP BY miss_type
),
late_suppliers AS (
  SELECT
    s.supplier_name,
    s.reliability_tier,
    COUNT(*) AS late_contributing_lines,
    AVG(po.late_days) AS avg_supplier_late_days
  FROM otif_analysis oa
  JOIN mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff ON oa.so_line_key = ff.so_line_key
  JOIN mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po ON oa.item_key = po.item_key AND ff.supplier_key = po.supplier_key
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
  WHERE oa.miss_type IN ('Late Only', 'Both Late and Short')
  GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.reliability_tier
  ORDER BY late_contributing_lines DESC
)
SELECT
  'Miss Type Breakdown' AS category,
  miss_type AS dimension,
  line_count AS count,
  pct_of_misses AS percentage
FROM miss_counts
UNION ALL
SELECT
  'Suppliers Contributing to Late Misses' AS category,
  supplier_name || ' (Tier ' || reliability_tier || ')' AS dimension,
  late_contributing_lines AS count,
  (late_contributing_lines * 100.0 / (SELECT SUM(late_contributing_lines) FROM late_suppliers)) AS percentage
FROM late_suppliers;
```

question: Which late suppliers are hurting our cash flow the most, and through which SKUs?
sql:
WITH item_supplier AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT po.item_key, po.supplier_key
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
),
supplier_cash_impact AS (
  SELECT 
    s.supplier_key,
    s.supplier_name,
    s.reliability_tier,
    AVG(po.late_days) AS avg_late_days,
    COUNT(DISTINCT po.po_line_key) AS po_line_count,
    AVG(CASE WHEN snap.is_stockout THEN 100.0 ELSE 0 END) AS stockout_pct,
    AVG(ff.order_to_ship_days) AS avg_order_to_ship_days,
    SUM(pa.days_to_pay * pa.amount_applied) / NULLIF(SUM(pa.amount_applied), 0) AS days_to_collect
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
  LEFT JOIN mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap USING (item_key)
  LEFT JOIN mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff USING (item_key)
  LEFT JOIN mro_distributor.ar_invoice i ON ff.customer_key = i.customer_key AND ff.ship_date_key = i.invoice_date_key
  LEFT JOIN item_supplier m ON ff.item_key = m.item_key
  LEFT JOIN mro_distributor.ar_payment_application pa ON i.invoice_key = pa.invoice_key
  GROUP BY s.supplier_key, s.supplier_name, s.reliability_tier
  HAVING AVG(po.late_days) > 0
),
supplier_item_impact AS (
  SELECT 
    s.supplier_key,
    s.supplier_name,
    s.reliability_tier,
    po.item_key,
    AVG(po.late_days) AS item_avg_late_days,
    COUNT(*) AS item_po_count,
    AVG(CASE WHEN snap.is_stockout THEN 100.0 ELSE 0 END) AS item_stockout_pct
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
  LEFT JOIN mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap USING (item_key)
  GROUP BY s.supplier_key, s.supplier_name, s.reliability_tier, po.item_key
  HAVING AVG(po.late_days) > 0
)
SELECT 
  sci.supplier_key,
  sci.supplier_name,
  sci.reliability_tier,
  sci.avg_late_days,
  sci.po_line_count,
  ROUND(sci.stockout_pct, 1) AS stockout_pct,
  ROUND(sci.avg_order_to_ship_days, 1) AS avg_order_to_ship_days,
  ROUND(sci.days_to_collect, 1) AS days_to_collect,
  sii.item_key,
  sii.item_po_count,
  ROUND(sii.item_stockout_pct, 1) AS item_stockout_pct
FROM supplier_cash_impact sci
JOIN supplier_item_impact sii ON sci.supplier_key = sii.supplier_key
ORDER BY sci.days_to_collect DESC NULLS LAST, sci.avg_late_days DESC, sii.item_key;

question: Explain whether our CCC problem is driven more by slow customers, excess inventory, or supplier payment timing.
sql:
WITH dso_calc AS (
  SELECT SUM(pa.days_to_pay * pa.amount_applied) / SUM(pa.amount_applied) AS dso_days
  FROM mro_distributor.ar_payment_application pa
),
dio_calc AS (
  SELECT 
    (SELECT AVG(daily_value) FROM (
       SELECT snap.snapshot_date_key, SUM(snap.value_on_hand) AS daily_value
       FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap
       GROUP BY snap.snapshot_date_key))
    /
    (SELECT SUM(sol.ext_cost) / COUNT(DISTINCT sol.order_date_key)
     FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol) AS dio_days
),
dpo_calc AS (
  SELECT SUM(bp.days_to_pay * bp.amount_paid) / SUM(bp.amount_paid) AS dpo_days
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_bill_payment bp
)
SELECT 
  dso.dso_days,
  dio.dio_days,
  dpo.dpo_days,
  dso.dso_days + dio.dio_days - dpo.dpo_days AS cash_conversion_cycle_days,
  CASE 
    WHEN dso.dso_days > dio.dio_days AND dso.dso_days > dpo.dpo_days THEN 'Slow Customers (DSO)'
    WHEN dio.dio_days > dso.dso_days AND dio.dio_days > dpo.dpo_days THEN 'Excess Inventory (DIO)'
    WHEN dpo.dpo_days < dso.dso_days AND dpo.dpo_days < dio.dio_days THEN 'Supplier Payment Timing (DPO)'
    ELSE 'Mixed drivers'
  END AS primary_ccc_driver
FROM dso_calc dso, dio_calc dio, dpo_calc dpo

```sql
SELECT 
  ds.subsidiary_name,
  ds.country,
  ROUND(dso.dso_days, 1) AS dso_days,
  ROUND(dio.dio_days, 1) AS dio_days,
  ROUND(dpo.dpo_days, 1) AS dpo_days,
  ROUND(dso.dso_days + dio.dio_days - dpo.dpo_days, 1) AS ccc_days,
  CASE 
    WHEN dso.dso_days >= dio.dio_days AND dso.dso_days >= dpo.dpo_days THEN 'DSO'
    WHEN dio.dio_days >= dso.dso_days AND dio.dio_days >= dpo.dpo_days THEN 'DIO'
    ELSE 'DPO'
  END AS largest_component
FROM mro_distributor.dim_subsidiary ds
CROSS JOIN (
  SELECT SUM(pa.days_to_pay * pa.amount_applied) / SUM(pa.amount_applied) AS dso_days
  FROM mro_distributor.ar_payment_application pa
) dso
CROSS JOIN (
  SELECT 
    (SELECT AVG(daily_value) FROM (
      SELECT snap.snapshot_date_key, SUM(snap.value_on_hand) AS daily_value
      FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap
      GROUP BY snap.snapshot_date_key))
    /
    (SELECT SUM(sol.ext_cost) / COUNT(DISTINCT sol.order_date_key)
     FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol) AS dio_days
) dio
CROSS JOIN (
  SELECT SUM(bp.days_to_pay * bp.amount_paid) / SUM(bp.amount_paid) AS dpo_days
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_bill_payment bp
) dpo
ORDER BY ccc_days DESC
```

question: Which suppliers indirectly delay customer cash collection through late receipts and delayed fulfillment?
sql:
WITH item_supplier AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT po.item_key, po.supplier_key
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
),
p2p AS (
  SELECT s.supplier_key, s.supplier_name, s.reliability_tier, AVG(po.late_days) AS avg_late_days
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
  GROUP BY s.supplier_key, s.supplier_name, s.reliability_tier
),
o2c AS (
  SELECT s.supplier_key, AVG(ff.order_to_ship_days) AS avg_order_to_ship_days
  FROM mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff
  JOIN item_supplier m USING (item_key)
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
  GROUP BY s.supplier_key
),
ar AS (
  SELECT s.supplier_key,
         SUM(pa.days_to_pay * pa.amount_applied) / SUM(pa.amount_applied) AS days_to_collect
  FROM mro_distributor.ar_invoice i
  JOIN mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff
    ON i.customer_key = ff.customer_key AND i.invoice_date_key = ff.ship_date_key
  JOIN item_supplier m ON ff.item_key = m.item_key
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
  JOIN mro_distributor.ar_payment_application pa USING (invoice_key)
  GROUP BY s.supplier_key
)
SELECT
  p2p.supplier_name,
  p2p.reliability_tier,
  p2p.avg_late_days,
  o2c.avg_order_to_ship_days,
  ar.days_to_collect
FROM p2p
LEFT JOIN o2c USING (supplier_key)
LEFT JOIN ar USING (supplier_key)
ORDER BY p2p.avg_late_days DESC, ar.days_to_collect DESC;

question: Show the top supplier-item pairs where late procurement caused stockouts and poor OTIF.
sql:
```sql
WITH item_supplier AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT po.item_key, po.supplier_key
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
),
supplier_lateness AS (
  SELECT 
    s.supplier_name,
    s.reliability_tier,
    po.item_key,
    po.supplier_key,
    AVG(po.late_days) AS avg_late_days,
    COUNT(*) AS po_count
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
  GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.reliability_tier, po.item_key, po.supplier_key
),
stockout_rates AS (
  SELECT 
    m.item_key,
    m.supplier_key,
    AVG(CASE WHEN snap.is_stockout THEN 100.0 ELSE 0 END) AS stockout_pct
  FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap
  JOIN item_supplier m USING (item_key)
  GROUP BY m.item_key, m.supplier_key
),
otif_by_item_supplier AS (
  SELECT 
    m.item_key,
    m.supplier_key,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN ff.ship_date_key IS NOT NULL
               AND ff.ship_date_key <= sol.promised_ship_date_key
               AND ff.qty_shipped >= sol.qty_ordered THEN 1 END) * 100.0
    / COUNT(*) AS otif_pct
  FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol
  LEFT JOIN mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff USING (so_line_key)
  JOIN item_supplier m ON ff.item_key = m.item_key
  GROUP BY m.item_key, m.supplier_key
)
SELECT 
  sl.supplier_name,
  sl.reliability_tier,
  sl.item_key,
  sl.avg_late_days,
  sl.po_count,
  COALESCE(sr.stockout_pct, 0) AS stockout_pct,
  COALESCE(oi.otif_pct, 0) AS otif_pct
FROM supplier_lateness sl
LEFT JOIN stockout_rates sr USING (item_key, supplier_key)
LEFT JOIN otif_by_item_supplier oi USING (item_key, supplier_key)
WHERE sl.avg_late_days > 0 
  AND (COALESCE(sr.stockout_pct, 0) > 0 OR COALESCE(oi.otif_pct, 0) < 100)
ORDER BY sl.avg_late_days DESC, COALESCE(sr.stockout_pct, 0) DESC, COALESCE(oi.otif_pct, 0) ASC
LIMIT 50;
```

question: Compare fast-moving versus slow-moving items: which group suffers more from supplier lateness?
sql:
```sql
WITH velocity AS (
  SELECT ff.item_key, SUM(ff.qty_shipped) AS units
  FROM mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff
  GROUP BY ff.item_key
),
cutoff AS (
  SELECT quantile_cont(units, 0.2) AS q FROM velocity
),
item_supplier AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT po.item_key, po.supplier_key
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
),
classification AS (
  SELECT
    v.item_key,
    CASE WHEN v.units <= cutoff.q THEN 'slow_moving' ELSE 'fast_moving' END AS item_class
  FROM velocity v, cutoff
),
supplier_lateness AS (
  SELECT
    c.item_class,
    AVG(po.late_days) AS avg_late_days,
    COUNT(*) AS po_count
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
  JOIN item_supplier m USING (item_key)
  JOIN classification c USING (item_key)
  GROUP BY c.item_class
)
SELECT
  item_class,
  avg_late_days,
  po_count
FROM supplier_lateness
ORDER BY item_class;
```

question: Which customers are hit twice: buying items from unreliable suppliers and experiencing our slowest fulfillment?
sql:
WITH item_supplier AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT po.item_key, po.supplier_key
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
),
unreliable_items AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT m.item_key
  FROM item_supplier m
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
  WHERE s.reliability_tier IN ('C', 'D')
),
slow_fulfillments AS (
  SELECT ff.customer_key, ff.item_key, ff.order_to_ship_days
  FROM mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff
  WHERE ff.order_to_ship_days >= (
    SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY order_to_ship_days)
    FROM mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line
  )
)
SELECT
  c.customer_key,
  c.customer_name,
  COUNT(DISTINCT sf.item_key) AS num_slow_unreliable_items,
  AVG(sf.order_to_ship_days) AS avg_order_to_ship_days,
  COUNT(DISTINCT sf.item_key) * 1.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT ff2.item_key) AS pct_unreliable_items_bought
FROM slow_fulfillments sf
JOIN unreliable_items ui USING (item_key)
JOIN mro_distributor.dim_customer c USING (customer_key)
JOIN mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff2 ON sf.customer_key = ff2.customer_key
  AND ui.item_key = ff2.item_key
GROUP BY c.customer_key, c.customer_name
ORDER BY num_slow_unreliable_items DESC;

question: Break down our OTIF misses: what share is late-only, short-only, or both, and which suppliers sit behind the late portion?
sql:
```sql
WITH otif_analysis AS (
  SELECT
    sol.so_line_key,
    sol.qty_ordered,
    ff.qty_shipped,
    ff.ship_date_key,
    sol.promised_ship_date_key,
    CASE 
      WHEN ff.ship_date_key IS NULL THEN 'Never Shipped'
      WHEN ff.ship_date_key > sol.promised_ship_date_key AND ff.qty_shipped < sol.qty_ordered THEN 'Both Late and Short'
      WHEN ff.ship_date_key > sol.promised_ship_date_key THEN 'Late Only'
      WHEN ff.qty_shipped < sol.qty_ordered THEN 'Short Only'
      ELSE 'On Time In Full'
    END AS miss_type,
    m.item_key,
    m.supplier_key
  FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol
  LEFT JOIN mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff USING (so_line_key)
  LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT DISTINCT po.item_key, po.supplier_key
    FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
  ) m USING (item_key)
),
miss_summary AS (
  SELECT
    miss_type,
    COUNT(*) AS line_count,
    COUNT(*) * 100.0 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM otif_analysis) AS pct_of_total,
    CASE WHEN miss_type LIKE '%Late%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS has_late_component
  FROM otif_analysis
  WHERE miss_type != 'On Time In Full'
  GROUP BY miss_type
),
late_suppliers AS (
  SELECT
    s.supplier_name,
    s.reliability_tier,
    COUNT(*) AS late_lines,
    COUNT(*) * 100.0 / (
      SELECT COUNT(*) FROM otif_analysis WHERE miss_type LIKE '%Late%'
    ) AS pct_of_late_misses
  FROM otif_analysis oa
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
  WHERE oa.miss_type LIKE '%Late%'
  GROUP BY s.supplier_name, s.reliability_tier
  ORDER BY late_lines DESC
)
SELECT
  'Miss Type Breakdown' AS analysis_type,
  miss_type AS dimension,
  line_count AS count,
  ROUND(pct_of_total, 2) AS pct_of_total_lines,
  NULL AS supplier_name,
  NULL AS tier
FROM miss_summary
UNION ALL
SELECT
  'Suppliers Behind Late Misses' AS analysis_type,
  supplier_name AS dimension,
  late_lines AS count,
  ROUND(pct_of_late_misses, 2) AS pct_of_total_lines,
  supplier_name,
  reliability_tier
FROM late_suppliers
ORDER BY analysis_type DESC, count DESC;
```

question: Which late suppliers are hurting our cash flow the most, and through which SKUs?
sql:
WITH item_supplier AS (

),
supplier_metrics AS (

  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)

item_metrics AS (
  SELECT 
    m.supplier_key,
    m.item_key,
    di.sku,
    di.item_name,
    di.category,
    COUNT(DISTINCT snap.snapshot_date_key) FILTER (WHERE snap.is_stockout) AS stockout_days,
    COUNT(DISTINCT snap.snapshot_date_key) AS total_days,
    AVG(CASE WHEN snap.is_stockout THEN 100.0 ELSE 0 END) AS stockout_pct,
    AVG(ff.order_to_ship_days) AS avg_order_to_ship_days
  FROM item_supplier m
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_item di USING (item_key)
  LEFT JOIN mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap USING (item_key)
  LEFT JOIN mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff USING (item_key)
  GROUP BY m.supplier_key, m.item_key, di.sku, di.item_name, di.category
)
SELECT 
  sm.supplier_name,
  sm.reliability_tier,
  sm.avg_late_days,
  sm.po_count,

  im.item_name,
  im.category,
  ROUND(im.stockout_pct, 1) AS stockout_pct,
  ROUND(im.avg_order_to_ship_days, 1) AS avg_order_to_ship_days
FROM supplier_metrics sm
JOIN item_metrics im USING (supplier_key)
WHERE sm.reliability_tier IN ('C', 'D')

question: Which subsidiaries have the highest CCC, and which component is responsible: DSO, DIO, or DPO?
sql:
```sql
WITH dso_by_subsidiary AS (
  SELECT 
    sub.subsidiary_key,
    sub.subsidiary_name,
    SUM(pa.days_to_pay * pa.amount_applied) / SUM(pa.amount_applied) AS dso
  FROM mro_distributor.ar_payment_application pa
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_subsidiary sub USING (subsidiary_key)
  GROUP BY sub.subsidiary_key, sub.subsidiary_name
),
dio_by_subsidiary AS (
  SELECT 
    sub.subsidiary_key,
    sub.subsidiary_name,
    (SELECT AVG(daily_value) FROM (
      SELECT snap.snapshot_date_key, SUM(snap.value_on_hand) AS daily_value
      FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap
      WHERE snap.subsidiary_key = sub.subsidiary_key
      GROUP BY snap.snapshot_date_key
    )) / (SELECT SUM(sol.ext_cost) / COUNT(DISTINCT sol.order_date_key)
          FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol
          WHERE sol.subsidiary_key = sub.subsidiary_key) AS dio
  FROM mro_distributor.dim_subsidiary sub
),
dpo_by_subsidiary AS (
  SELECT 
    sub.subsidiary_key,
    sub.subsidiary_name,
    SUM(bp.days_to_pay * bp.amount_paid) / SUM(bp.amount_paid) AS dpo
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_bill_payment bp
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_subsidiary sub USING (subsidiary_key)
  GROUP BY sub.subsidiary_key, sub.subsidiary_name
)
SELECT 
  dso.subsidiary_name,
  ROUND(dso.dso, 1) AS dso_days,
  ROUND(dio.dio, 1) AS dio_days,
  ROUND(dpo.dpo, 1) AS dpo_days,
  ROUND(dso.dso + dio.dio - dpo.dpo, 1) AS ccc_days
FROM dso_by_subsidiary dso
JOIN dio_by_subsidiary dio USING (subsidiary_key)
JOIN dpo_by_subsidiary dpo USING (subsidiary_key)
ORDER BY ccc_days DESC;
```

```sql
WITH item_supplier AS (

supplier_lateness AS (

    COUNT(*) AS po_count
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po

  FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap

  GROUP BY m.supplier_key, m.item_key
),
otif_metrics AS (
  SELECT 
    m.supplier_key,
    m.item_key,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN ff.ship_date_key IS NOT NULL 
               AND ff.ship_date_key <= sol.promised_ship_date_key 
               AND ff.qty_shipped >= sol.qty_ordered THEN 1 END) * 100.0 
    / COUNT(*) AS otif_pct
  FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol
  LEFT JOIN mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff USING (so_line_key)
  JOIN item_supplier m ON sol.item_key = m.item_key
  GROUP BY m.supplier_key, m.item_key
)
SELECT 
  s.supplier_name,
  i.item_name,
  i.sku,
  sl.avg_late_days,
  sm.stockout_pct,
  om.otif_pct,
  sl.po_count
FROM supplier_lateness sl
JOIN stockout_metrics sm USING (supplier_key, item_key)
JOIN otif_metrics om USING (supplier_key, item_key)
JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s ON sl.supplier_key = s.supplier_key
JOIN mro_distributor.dim_item i ON sl.item_key = i.item_key
WHERE sl.avg_late_days > 0 
  AND sm.stockout_pct > 0
  AND om.otif_pct < 90
ORDER BY sl.avg_late_days DESC, sm.stockout_pct DESC, om.otif_pct ASC
```

sql:
```sql
WITH item_supplier AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT po.item_key, po.supplier_key
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
),
supplier_lateness AS (
  SELECT 
    po.item_key,
    s.supplier_name,
    s.reliability_tier,
    AVG(po.late_days) AS avg_late_days
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
  GROUP BY po.item_key, s.supplier_name, s.reliability_tier
),
stockout_metrics AS (
  SELECT 
    snap.item_key,
    AVG(CASE WHEN snap.is_stockout THEN 100.0 ELSE 0 END) AS stockout_pct,
    COUNT(*) AS snapshot_days
  FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap
  GROUP BY snap.item_key
),
item_revenue AS (
  SELECT 
    sol.item_key,
    SUM(sol.ext_amount) AS total_revenue,
    COUNT(DISTINCT sol.so_number) AS order_count
  FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol
  GROUP BY sol.item_key
),
item_fulfillment AS (
  SELECT 
    ff.item_key,
    AVG(ff.order_to_ship_days) AS avg_order_to_ship_days
  FROM mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff
  GROUP BY ff.item_key
)
SELECT 
  sl.item_key,
  di.sku,
  di.item_name,
  di.category,
  sl.supplier_name,
  sl.reliability_tier,
  sl.avg_late_days,
  ROUND(sm.stockout_pct, 1) AS stockout_pct,
  ROUND(ir.total_revenue, 0) AS total_revenue,
  ir.order_count,
  ROUND(ifulfill.avg_order_to_ship_days, 1) AS avg_order_to_ship_days,
  ROUND(sl.avg_late_days * sm.stockout_pct * ir.total_revenue / 1000, 0) AS risk_score
FROM supplier_lateness sl
JOIN stockout_metrics sm ON sl.item_key = sm.item_key
JOIN item_revenue ir ON sl.item_key = ir.item_key
JOIN item_fulfillment ifulfill ON sl.item_key = ifulfill.item_key
JOIN mro_distributor.dim_item di ON sl.item_key = di.item_key
WHERE sl.reliability_tier IN ('C', 'D')
  AND sm.stockout_pct > 5
  AND ir.total_revenue > 10000
ORDER BY risk_score DESC
LIMIT 50;
```

question: Compare fast-moving versus slow-moving items: which group suffers more from supplier lateness?
Did Q4 revenue seasonality worsen stockouts, and which suppliers failed to keep up with the demand spike?
Which customers are hit twice: buying items from unreliable suppliers and experiencing our slowest fulfillment?
sql:

```sql
WITH item_supplier AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT po.item_key, po.supplier_key
  FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po
),
unreliable_items AS (
  SELECT m.item_key
  FROM item_supplier m
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
  WHERE s.reliability_tier IN ('C', 'D')
),
slow_fulfillment AS (
  SELECT ff.customer_key, AVG(ff.order_to_ship_days) AS avg_ship_days
  FROM mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff
  GROUP BY ff.customer_key
  HAVING AVG(ff.order_to_ship_days) >= (
    SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY avg_days)
    FROM (
      SELECT AVG(order_to_ship_days) AS avg_days
      FROM mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line
      GROUP BY customer_key
    )
  )
),
customers_buying_unreliable AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT ff.customer_key
  FROM mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff
  JOIN unreliable_items ui ON ff.item_key = ui.item_key
)
SELECT
  c.customer_key,
  c.customer_name,
  c.segment,
  c.region,
  sf.avg_ship_days
FROM customers_buying_unreliable cbu
JOIN slow_fulfillment sf ON cbu.customer_key = sf.customer_key
JOIN mro_distributor.dim_customer c ON cbu.customer_key = c.customer_key
ORDER BY sf.avg_ship_days DESC
```

question: Break down our OTIF misses: what share is late-only, short-only, or both, and which suppliers sit behind the late portion?
sql:
```sql
WITH otif_analysis AS (
  SELECT
    sol.so_line_key,
    sol.qty_ordered,
    sol.promised_ship_date_key,
    COALESCE(ff.ship_date_key, NULL) AS actual_ship_date_key,
    COALESCE(ff.qty_shipped, 0) AS qty_shipped,
    COALESCE(ff.item_key, sol.item_key) AS item_key,
    CASE 
      WHEN ff.ship_date_key IS NULL THEN 'Never Shipped'
      WHEN ff.ship_date_key > sol.promised_ship_date_key AND ff.qty_shipped < sol.qty_ordered THEN 'Both Late and Short'
      WHEN ff.ship_date_key > sol.promised_ship_date_key THEN 'Late Only'
      WHEN ff.qty_shipped < sol.qty_ordered THEN 'Short Only'
      ELSE 'OTIF'
    END AS miss_type
  FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol
  LEFT JOIN mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff USING (so_line_key)
),
miss_summary AS (
  SELECT
    miss_type,
    COUNT(*) AS line_count,
    COUNT(*) * 100.0 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM otif_analysis WHERE miss_type != 'OTIF') AS pct_of_misses
  FROM otif_analysis
  WHERE miss_type != 'OTIF'
  GROUP BY miss_type
),
late_suppliers AS (
  SELECT
    oa.miss_type,
    s.supplier_name,
    s.reliability_tier,
    COUNT(DISTINCT oa.so_line_key) AS late_lines,
    COUNT(DISTINCT oa.so_line_key) * 100.0 / 
      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM otif_analysis WHERE miss_type IN ('Late Only', 'Both Late and Short')) 
      AS pct_of_late_misses
  FROM otif_analysis oa
  JOIN mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol ON oa.so_line_key = sol.so_line_key
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_item di ON oa.item_key = di.item_key
  JOIN mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po ON di.item_key = po.item_key
  JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s ON po.supplier_key = s.supplier_key
  WHERE oa.miss_type IN ('Late Only', 'Both Late and Short')
  GROUP BY oa.miss_type, s.supplier_name, s.reliability_tier
  ORDER BY oa.miss_type, late_lines DESC
)
SELECT 'Miss Type Summary' AS section, NULL AS supplier_name, NULL AS reliability_tier, line_count, pct_of_misses FROM miss_summary
UNION ALL
SELECT 'Suppliers Behind Late Misses' AS section, late_suppliers.supplier_name, late_suppliers.reliability_tier, late_lines, pct_of_late_misses FROM late_suppliers;
```
